author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text: Muscles don't work like that AT ALL, for s start they are not pneumatic. The 'bunching' of muscles is just a side effect of the contraction. Yes the pneumatic actuator does work, but its NOT muscular. Muscular contraction is more akin to rods ratcheting past each other. You can get a similar contracting 'muscle' using screw-thread actuators, they are surprisingly powerful, and often quite rapid. You could also power them with a rotary air-tool motor.

author: @TheBaconWizard
text: Have you seen how much the "proper" version of these as-sold by FESTO robotics cost?? An absolute fortune!!! You can do a quite decent version just using braided hose (kevlar best, but any will do), latex rubber tubing and hose-clamps (actuated with solenoid valves and an air-pump) Remarkably strong and fast-acting and LIGHT, which gives them a good power:weight ratio; their main advantage.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yep - they are stupid money and tis is the principle - you are spot on get better tube and kevlar wrap and you will have an amazing soft actuator
